description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Deliverable:
Add test/test_support/state_json_fixture.ex with helpers seed/2, read/1, assert_state_contains/3 to drive multi-trajectory state.json fixtures (cold/warm/seeded/dangling). Wire test/test_support into mix.exs elixirc_paths (test env only) and into test_helper.exs.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: existing test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs continues to pass.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: at least one new test in hash_store_test.exs (or a smoke test in a new test file) uses StateJsonFixture to seed and read state.json contents in a tmp root.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**
- .spec/**
- priv/**
Allowed touches:
- test/test_support/state_json_fixture.ex
- mix.exs (elixirc_paths only)
- test/test_helper.exs

Out of Scope (intent):
- No production code changes.
- No modification of HashStore semantics (that's S1).
- No modification of existing test fixtures.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Summary: Adds SpecLedEx.StateJsonFixture under test/test_support/ to drive multi-trajectory state.json fixtures (cold/warm/seeded/dangling) for downstream stages. The fixture sits on top of HashStore.write/2 / read/1 — seed/2 hex-encodes raw binary hashes and stamps the current hasher_version, read/1 delegates to HashStore.read/1, and assert_state_contains/3 raises ExUnit.AssertionError with diagnostic context. mix.exs gains a test-env `elixirc_paths(:test)` clause appending "test/test_support" to the lib paths so the helper is compiled into the project app under MIX_ENV=test only; production builds remain unchanged. No production code changes; no .spec/** changes; no modification of HashStore semantics or existing test fixtures (legacy test_support/ at the repo root is untouched and continues to load via Code.require_file/2).

- Requirements with passing tests: N/A for this stage. The Advances target is specled.realized_by, but per the Verification clause this is test-infra only; no requirement-level tests are claimed to be added by S0. The 39 requirement_without_test_tag findings are unchanged from base ref abeee1e (verified via state.json findings count).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - "No production code changes": confirmed (mix.exs is the only non-test file; change is elixirc_paths-only, gated on Mix.env() == :test, with a pure refactor extracting lib_paths/0 — production builds compile the same lib paths).
  - "No modification of HashStore semantics": confirmed (lib/specled_ex/realization/hash_store.ex is untouched; HashStore.merge/2 is NOT introduced here — that's S1).
  - "No modification of existing test fixtures": confirmed (test_support/specled_ex_case.ex, test_support/append_only_fixtures.ex, test_support/specled_ex_integration_case.ex all untouched; test_helper.exs change is comments + trailing :ok only).
- Project verification:
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors (dev): clean
  - MIX_ENV=test mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
  - mix test (full suite): 2 properties, 669 tests, 0 failures
  - mix test test/specled_ex/state_json_fixture_test.exs test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs: 19 tests, 0 failures
  - mix spec.check: 39 findings (errors=19 warnings=20) — IDENTICAL to base ref abeee1ead81ae61b1438f8b8ef7d955310f60901 (verified via git show abeee1e:.spec/state.json | jq '.findings | length' = 39). All findings are pre-existing, attributable to downstream stage requirements (S1-S6) and to the mix_tasks.spec.md surface for spec.dedup_realized_by.

Source-claim trace (test-infra):
- StateJsonFixture.seed/2: hex-encodes raw binary hashes via Base.encode16/2 (case: :lower), stamps HashStore.hasher_version/0 (currently 1), then calls HashStore.write/2 with the encoded realization map. HashStore.write/2 ensure_version/1 is a no-op when "hasher_version" is already present (verified at lib/specled_ex/realization/hash_store.ex:192-195) — so the fixture's stamped entries pass through unchanged.
- StateJsonFixture.read/1: delegates to HashStore.read/1, returning the same tier → key → entry shape.
- StateJsonFixture.assert_state_contains/3: raises ExUnit.AssertionError with diagnostic context naming present-tiers / present-keys.
- The smoke test "produces an on-disk shape that HashStore.read/1 understands" round-trips through HashStore.read/1 (not just the fixture's own read/1), confirming on-disk compatibility.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- TENSION (worth flagging to the auditor): the ticket's "Allowed touches" enumerates exactly three paths (mix.exs / test/test_helper.exs / test/test_support/state_json_fixture.ex) but the Verification clause explicitly authorizes "a smoke test in a new test file" — and the obvious place for that smoke test (test/specled_ex/state_json_fixture_test.exs) is not in the Allowed list. Treating the Verification clause as authoritative for this stage (per the auditor instructions), the new test file is in-scope. If the auditor reads "Allowed touches" as a hard whitelist, the smoke test file is technically a scope violation despite being explicitly required by the spec's own Verification clause. I'm calling this PASS because the Verification clause unambiguously calls for the file and "Out of Scope (files)" does not list test/specled_ex/**.
- The two execute:true tagged_tests groups in realized_by.spec.md cover requirements whose tests will land in later stages (S2 schema_shape/meta_field/etc., S3 effective_binding_*). They were already flipped to execute:true at base ref abeee1e (the distillation commit) and produce requirement_without_test_tag findings; this stage neither introduced nor resolved that condition.
- mix.exs change is genuinely scoped to elixirc_paths: arity widened from 0 → 1 (gated on Mix.env()), :test clause appends "test/test_support" to the existing lib paths, and lib_paths/0 is a private helper extracted purely for readability of the per-env clauses. No tracer or compile-options changes.
- The legacy test_support/ at the repo root (specled_ex_case.ex, append_only_fixtures.ex, specled_ex_integration_case.ex) is untouched and continues to load via Code.require_file/2 in test_helper.exs as before.
